'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue ware and Haji ware, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.

'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue ware and Haji ware, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.

'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue ware and Haji ware, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.

== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue ware and Haji ware, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.

'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue ware and Haji ware, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.

The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.

Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.

'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|土師器}}" to "|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}"
revision.text:
'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群}}

The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} aligned on an east-west axis. Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.

revision.text:
'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群}}

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=draft}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|12=draft}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=draft}}

== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.

'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|12=draft|12=draft}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.

[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|12=draft|12=draft}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = カンス塚古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = 平荘湖古墳群 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[加古川市]]平荘町池尻 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 47|緯度秒 = 33.30 |経度度 = 134|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 46.00 |ISO = JP-28 |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]] |規模 = |埋葬施設 = [[竪穴式石室]]
（竪穴系横口式石室?）
（内部に木棺か） |出土品 = 金製垂飾付耳飾ほか副葬品多数・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 現在は水没 |地図 = Japan Hyogo |アイコン = 帆立貝形古墳-西 |ラベル = カンス塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''カンス塚古墳'''（かんすづかこふん）は、[[兵庫県]][[加古川市]]平荘町池尻にあった[[古墳]]。平荘湖古墳群を構成した古墳の1つ。現在では平荘湖底に水没している。 == 概要 == 兵庫県南部、平荘湖の形成前に存在した盆地の西寄りに築造された古墳である。墳丘・石室の一部が破壊されているほか、[[1963年|1963]]・[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]38・41年）に発掘調査が実施され、1966年に平荘湖底に没している。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向けた。後円部は直径約30メートルを測った{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]・[[円筒埴輪]]列が検出されている{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]で、石室主軸を東西方向としたが、竪穴系横口式石室の可能性もある{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。石室内の調査では、金製垂飾付耳飾のほか銅鏡・装身具・武器・武具・農工具・[[須恵器]]（TK216型式期）・[[土師器]]などが検出されている。副葬品のなかでも金製垂飾付耳飾は朝鮮半島からの移入品と推測され、また鉄製鍛造具・砥石は製鉄との関連を示唆しており、いずれも渡来色の濃い様相として注目される{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 出土品は[[1990年]]（[[平成]]2年）に加古川市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1963年]]（[[昭和]]38年）、第1次調査（加古川市教育委員会、[[1985年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）、第2次調査。調査後に水没（加古川市教育委員会、1985年に概報刊行）。 * [[1990年]]（[[平成]]2年）10月11日、出土品が加古川市指定有形文化財に指定。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されている。石室主軸を東西方向とし、長さ約4.4メートル・東端幅約1.2メートル・西端約1メートル・高さ約0.9メートルを測った{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 石室の壁は、割石状の塊石を垂直に積んで構築される。床面には板石を全面に敷くが、排水のために中央部がわずかに高く、側壁側に緩やかに傾斜して蒲鉾形を呈する。天井石は7枚で、東側5枚が遺存する。なお現在残る写真によれば、竪穴系横口式石室であった可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 石室内では、石室中央部において木棺を東枕で据え、周りに副葬品を配置する。石室の中央部では耳飾1対と玉類が出土しており、着装状態と見られる。木棺・東壁の間に須恵器群、南東隅に鉄鉾、南壁・北壁に沿って刀剣類・鉄鏃を置く。石室西半部は撹乱されているが、木棺・西壁の間には短甲、北西隅に鍛造具・工具が認められる。以上のほか、天井石の上で銅鏡が検出されている{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:カンス塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|加古川総合文化センター博物館展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:カンス塚古墳出土 金製耳飾.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|金製垂飾付耳飾}}]] [[ファイル:カンス塚古墳出土 銅鏡・装身具・砥石.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|銅鏡・装身具・砥石}}]] 石室内の調査で検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 * 銅鏡 ** 変形四獣鏡 1 * 装身具 ** 金製垂飾付耳飾 1対 ** 管玉 14 ** ガラス小玉 多数 * 武器 ** 鉄刀 9 ** 鉄剣 1 ** 鉄鉾 3 ** 鉄鏃 7群 ** 胡簶金具 2 * 武具 ** 短甲 - 横矧板鋲留式。 ** 頸甲 ** 草摺 * 農工具 ** 鉄鎌 1 ** 小型鉄鎌 4 ** 鉄斧 1 ** 小型鉄鑿 2 ** 鉇 2 ** 刀子 2 ** 鏨 1 ** 鉄鉗 1 ** 鉄槌 1 ** 鉸具 2 * [[須恵器]] - 高坏3、壺3、把手付埦1、𤭯1。 * [[土師器]] - 壺1。 * 鎹 * 砥石 1 副葬品には馬具が含まれない点が注意される{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 ファイル:カンス塚古墳出土 鉄製品.JPG|鉄製品 ファイル:カンス塚古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|須恵器 == 文化財 == === 加古川市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** カンス塚古墳出土品一括 24点（考古資料） - 1990年（平成2年）10月11日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 加古川総合文化センター博物館（加古川市平岡町新在家） - カンス塚古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=カンス塚古墳|title=兵庫県史 考古資料編|publisher=兵庫県|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|兵庫県史 考古資料編|1992}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=カンス塚古墳|title=加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|publisher=加古川市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=岡崎正雄|year=1989|chapter=加古川工業用水ダム古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|加古川工業用水ダム古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=平荘湖古墳群 > カンス塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 29 兵庫県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|カンス塚古墳（平凡社）|1999}}}} ==関連文献== {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=|title=加古川市カンス塚古墳発掘調査概要|publisher=加古川市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[平荘湖古墳群]] {{デフォルトソート:かんすつかこふん}} [[Category:兵庫県の古墳]] [[Category:加古川市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

（竪穴系横口式石室?）
（内部に木棺か） |出土品 = 金製垂飾付耳飾ほか副葬品多数・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 現在は水没 |地図 = Japan Hyogo |アイコン = 帆立貝形古墳-西 |ラベル = カンス塚
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''カンス塚古墳'''（かんすづかこふん）は、[[兵庫県]][[加古川市]]平荘町池尻にあった[[古墳]]。平荘湖古墳群を構成した古墳の1つ。現在では平荘湖底に水没している。 == 概要 == 兵庫県南部、平荘湖の形成前に存在した盆地の西寄りに築造された古墳である。墳丘・石室の一部が破壊されているほか、[[1963年|1963]]・[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]38・41年）に発掘調査が実施され、1966年に平荘湖底に没している。 墳形は、前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、前方部を西方向に向けた。後円部は直径約30メートルを測った{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。墳丘表面では[[葺石]]・[[円筒埴輪]]列が検出されている{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。埋葬施設は[[竪穴式石室]]で、石室主軸を東西方向としたが、竪穴系横口式石室の可能性もある{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。石室内の調査では、金製垂飾付耳飾のほか銅鏡・装身具・武器・武具・農工具・[[須恵器]]（TK216型式期）・[[土師器]]などが検出されている。副葬品のなかでも金製垂飾付耳飾は朝鮮半島からの移入品と推測され、また鉄製鍛造具・砥石は製鉄との関連を示唆しており、いずれも渡来色の濃い様相として注目される{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]中期の[[5世紀]]中葉頃と推定される{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 出土品は[[1990年]]（[[平成]]2年）に加古川市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1963年]]（[[昭和]]38年）、第1次調査（加古川市教育委員会、[[1985年]]に概報刊行）。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）、第2次調査。調査後に水没（加古川市教育委員会、1985年に概報刊行）。 * [[1990年]]（[[平成]]2年）10月11日、出土品が加古川市指定有形文化財に指定。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設としては[[竪穴式石室]]が構築されている。石室主軸を東西方向とし、長さ約4.4メートル・東端幅約1.2メートル・西端約1メートル・高さ約0.9メートルを測った{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 石室の壁は、割石状の塊石を垂直に積んで構築される。床面には板石を全面に敷くが、排水のために中央部がわずかに高く、側壁側に緩やかに傾斜して蒲鉾形を呈する。天井石は7枚で、東側5枚が遺存する。なお現在残る写真によれば、竪穴系横口式石室であった可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 石室内では、石室中央部において木棺を東枕で据え、周りに副葬品を配置する。石室の中央部では耳飾1対と玉類が出土しており、着装状態と見られる。木棺・東壁の間に須恵器群、南東隅に鉄鉾、南壁・北壁に沿って刀剣類・鉄鏃を置く。石室西半部は撹乱されているが、木棺・西壁の間には短甲、北西隅に鍛造具・工具が認められる。以上のほか、天井石の上で銅鏡が検出されている{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:カンス塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|加古川総合文化センター博物館展示（他画像も同様）。}}]] [[ファイル:カンス塚古墳出土 金製耳飾.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|金製垂飾付耳飾}}]] [[ファイル:カンス塚古墳出土 銅鏡・装身具・砥石.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|銅鏡・装身具・砥石}}]] 石室内の調査で検出された副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 * 銅鏡 ** 変形四獣鏡 1 * 装身具 ** 金製垂飾付耳飾 1対 ** 管玉 14 ** ガラス小玉 多数 * 武器 ** 鉄刀 9 ** 鉄剣 1 ** 鉄鉾 3 ** 鉄鏃 7群 ** 胡簶金具 2 * 武具 ** 短甲 - 横矧板鋲留式。 ** 頸甲 ** 草摺 * 農工具 ** 鉄鎌 1 ** 小型鉄鎌 4 ** 鉄斧 1 ** 小型鉄鑿 2 ** 鉇 2 ** 刀子 2 ** 鏨 1 ** 鉄鉗 1 ** 鉄槌 1 ** 鉸具 2 * [[須恵器]] - 高坏3、壺3、把手付埦1、𤭯1。 * [[土師器]] - 壺1。 * 鎹 * 砥石 1 副葬品には馬具が含まれない点が注意される{{Sfn|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}。 ファイル:カンス塚古墳出土 鉄製品.JPG|鉄製品 ファイル:カンス塚古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|須恵器 == 文化財 == === 加古川市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** カンス塚古墳出土品一括 24点（考古資料） - 1990年（平成2年）10月11日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 加古川総合文化センター博物館（加古川市平岡町新在家） - カンス塚古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=カンス塚古墳|title=兵庫県史 考古資料編|publisher=兵庫県|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|兵庫県史 考古資料編|1992}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1996|chapter=カンス塚古墳|title=加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|publisher=加古川市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|加古川市史 第4巻 史料編I|1996}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=岡崎正雄|year=1989|chapter=加古川工業用水ダム古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|加古川工業用水ダム古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=平荘湖古墳群 > カンス塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 29 兵庫県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|カンス塚古墳（平凡社）|1999}}}} ==関連文献== {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=|title=加古川市カンス塚古墳発掘調査概要|publisher=加古川市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[平荘湖古墳群]] {{デフォルトソート:かんすつかこふん}} [[Category:兵庫県の古墳]] [[Category:加古川市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|12=draft|12=draft}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft|12=draft}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft|12=draft}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|12=draft|12=draft}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}

It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|qq=}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style horizontal stone chamber|qq=}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyogo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyogo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|qq=}}

'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|qq=}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/5th century|qq=}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}.

The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style horizontal stone chamber|qq=}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.

The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.

The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style horizontal stone chamber|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyōgo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyōgo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

revision.text:
'''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[Kakogawa]], [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style horizontal stone chamber|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyōgo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyōgo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kakogawa|qid=Q750569}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q122375889]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q122375889]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[:en:Kakogawa|Kakogawa]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505743}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style horizontal stone chamber|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyōgo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyōgo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505743}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|カンス塚古墳|version=99995565|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kansuzuka Kofun]][[ja:カンス塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kakogawa|WD=Q750569}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q122375889]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q122375889]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[:en:Kakogawa|Kakogawa]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505743}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style horizontal stone chamber|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.

The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
