The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.

The mound's construction is dated to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kansuzuka Kofun]][[ja:カンス塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kakogawa|WD=Q750569}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q122375889]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q122375889]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[:en:Kakogawa|Kakogawa]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505743}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyōgo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyōgo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505743}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|カンス塚古墳|version=99995565|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kansuzuka Kofun]][[ja:カンス塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kansuzuka Kofun]][[ja:カンス塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kansuzuka Kofun]][[ja:カンス塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kansuzuka Kofun]][[ja:カンス塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Kakogawa|WD=Q750569}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q122375889]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q122375889]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kansuzuka Kofun''' (カンス塚古墳) was a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Ikegiri, Heisocho, [[:en:Kakogawa|Kakogawa]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505743}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.
== Overview == The Kansuzuka Kofun was constructed in the western basin of what is now Heiso Lake. It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-[[:en:5th century|5th century]] during the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]].
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyōgo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyōgo Prefecture, 1992.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505743}}

The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The circular portion measured approximately 30 meters in diameter.
Surface findings included {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} and a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} (earthenware artifacts). The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis. The chamber yielded numerous artifacts, including gold pendant earrings, bronze mirrors, weaponry, agricultural tools, and ceramics such as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}}. Some items, such as the gold earrings, are believed to have been imported from the Korean Peninsula, highlighting cultural exchanges. The mound's construction is dated to the mid-{{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}.
Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun are designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City.
== Burial Chamber == The chamber's dimensions were approximately 4.4 meters in length, with widths of 1.2 meters at the east end and 1 meter at the west end, and a height of 0.9 meters. The floor was paved with flat stones, sloped gently for drainage. The walls were built using upright stones, and the ceiling was made of seven flat stones, five of which remain. Photographic evidence suggests the chamber might have been of the {{ill|pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja|竪穴系横口式石室|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style horizontal stone chamber|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} type.
A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyōgo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyōgo Prefecture, 1992.

A wooden coffin was positioned with its head to the east, and various grave goods were arranged around it, including weapons along the walls and ceremonial tools near the coffin. A bronze mirror was discovered on top of the ceiling stones.
== Excavation History == * 1963: The first excavation was conducted by the Kakogawa City Board of Education. * 1966: A second excavation took place before the site was submerged. * 1990: Artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun were designated as Tangible Cultural Properties by Kakogawa City.
== Artifacts == Key artifacts recovered include: * '''Bronze mirrors''': One irregularly shaped four-animal mirror. * '''Gold pendant earrings''': One pair. * '''Beads''': Including 14 tubular beads and numerous glass beads. * '''Weapons''': Iron swords, spearheads, and arrowheads. * '''Armor''': Including a short armor plate and neck guards. * '''Tools''': Iron tools such as sickles, chisels, and axes. * '''Ceramics''': Sue pottery and Haji pottery, including pedestaled dishes and jars.
The absence of horse-related artifacts is notable among the grave goods.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Kansuzuka Kofun Artifacts''': Designated as Tangible Cultural Properties of Kakogawa City on October 11, 1990.
== Related Facilities == * Kakogawa Comprehensive Cultural Center Museum: Houses and displays artifacts from Kansuzuka Kofun.
== References == * Kakogawa City History, Volume 4: Historical Records I, Kakogawa City, 1996. * Hyōgo Prefecture Archaeological Materials, Hyōgo Prefecture, 1992.

It was part of the {{ill|Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja|平荘湖古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heiso Lake Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505743|1=Heisō Lake Kofun Group}} and is now submerged under the Heiso Lake reservoir.

It was excavated twice, in 1963 and 1966, prior to being submerged following the formation of the reservoir. The mound was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with a shortened front and a circular rear section. The burial chamber was a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} aligned on an east-west axis.
